title: scripts/orchestrator/* function as canonical test surface + executable specification of extension surface requirements
context: POC TypeScript scripts under scripts/orchestrator/ (extract-mandates, extract-decs, extract-feedback, build-subagent-preamble, file-block-item, build-explore-brief landed at HEAD fa59884) were initially framed as orchestrator-side ergonomics wrappers replacing hand-construction variance in subagent briefs. Observed during their construction: each script's implementation surfaces a concrete gap in the extension surface — extract-feedback exposes memory-vs-substrate boundary violation (closed by FGAP-033 candidate); extract-mandates exposes mandate substrate-canon home gap; extract-decs exposes per-item macros library incompleteness (REVIEW-001); file-block-item exposes schema-aware filing UX gap that pre-existed for both Claude-Code orchestrator AND in-pi JIT agents; build-subagent-preamble exposes per-type context composition gap (DEC-0017 context-contracts territory). The scripts thereby double as both consumers of the canonical surface AND executable specifications of what the canonical surface must offer for agents to execute their work.
decision: scripts/orchestrator/*.ts have a CANONICAL DUAL ROLE per DEC-0019: (1) Claude-Code-side ergonomics wrappers around block-api / extension surface (per DEC-0014/0016 dual-surface pattern); AND (2) executable specifications of extension surface requirements that surface gaps via the act of being written. New scripts under scripts/orchestrator/ are evaluated on both axes: do they replace hand-construction variance? + do they surface gaps that become FGAPs driving extension implementation? Each gap surfaced is filed as FGAP (or referenced as existing FGAP) before script lands. Scripts are not throwaway POCs — they are persistent test surface for the extension API contract.
consequences: Every new script under scripts/orchestrator/ is reviewed for which extension primitives it consumes + which it forces into existence. The list goes in the script's header docstring as the SURFACED-GAPS section.

FGAPs surfaced by scripts are first-class — not informational notes in commit bodies. Each gets file-block-item invocation to file in framework-gaps.json with cross-reference to the script that surfaced it.

Tool-surface gap audit (analysis/2026-05-10-tool-surface-gap-audit.md) becomes partially auto-populated by the script-writing process — scripts that need primitives not yet a Pi tool = confirmed gap in the audit's scope.

Test surface property: when a Pi tool / block-api primitive / DispatchContext shape changes, the affected scripts are the canary — their breakage signals consumer-facing impact. Includes explicit script-to-consumer-need mapping kept current as scripts are added.
Dual-direction value drives priority: a gap that blocks BOTH a script AND a Pi tool consumer = higher priority than one that blocks only one direction.

Articulation of extension requirements becomes Claude-Code-readable in addition to schema-readable. A future Claude session reading scripts/orchestrator/ has a working executable map of what the extension surface offers vs needs.

Per the just-landed dual-surface pattern (feedback_orchestrator_scripts_dual_surface.md): library + Pi tool + CLI script land as a unit. DEC-0019 adds: the CLI script ALSO articulates the API contract requirement set surfaced during its writing — these are inputs to FGAP filings + tool-surface audit refresh.
